reviews.review_msg: Really sweet and easy to talk to professor, and easy class, quizzes and test are purely memorization of dates, people and techniques. Please laugh at his jokes even if you don't understand them.

Extremely professional, friendly, and amazing, well-put-together lectures. He knows how to lecture without only reading what he has on his slides.

He has a policy that restricts electronics use for note-taking, this was annoying at first but led to a very interactive and attentive environment during lectures. A definite positive.

This professor is one of the best professors you can get. Very professional, clean, reasonable, and passionate. He understands the structure of his lecture and class very clearly and teaches things in an intriguing way with great tips and lessons on how you can keep handling academic writing or study in the future as well.
He is honestly one of the best profs I have ever had. His lectures are entertaining, his assignments are easy if you keep up with the readings, and he has been super accomodating during the pandemic.

Genuinely wonderful. So funny & memorable. I can't recommend him enough. He made the online format SO doable: omitting some assignments, making tutorials asynchronous, giving clear learning aids, etc. While he does assign a lot of readings, they're truly enlightening, and he explains them in class. Just 1 short non-research paper & 2 easy MC exams.

HE IS THE BEST. If you haven't taken the class CA 136 yet, please do it. It's worth it. He is so passionate about his work and he explains everything so clearly. Not to mention he is very chill and funny. I took this course online during COVID and even though it was online it was still one of my favourite classes this semester. Should be mandatory.

Great guy. Understanding and chill. If you're having trouble with a concept or need an extension, just ask and he's happy to help. Sometimes I got bored in class but that was more to do with my short attention span than his teacher. He's a good pick. P.S. He may go on a rant about hating Tom Hanks once or twice!

Love Pavsek! His lectures were clear and to the point while also relating to both the textbook and film screenings. I felt like I actually learned in this class, instead of just getting credit. Plus his anecdotes are interesting and funny.

Chris is a hilarious professor in a very sarcastic way! His classes are very fun! We view a film nearly every week! Make sure you attend the classes, and take notes, since most of the midterm/final questions are from there! Do read the canvas readings, (they will be on the exam). We also had two 3 to 4 pages papers to write, so start early! Enjoy!

Almost every class we would watch a movie. Class not mandatory but if you miss one it's hard to catch up because there are no recordings. A great beginners course to film or arts in general. Really kind. I was only on the waiting list for this course until I talked to Chris and he personally helped me get in. Textbook readings not mandatory at all.
